title: Workspace Output Policy Migration

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.

post_decomposition_delegation.route skipped reason: Implemented locally because the bounded slice is cohesive around command-generation output policy, generated facades, static checker metrics, and one primitive conformance test; delegating after implementation would not reduce proof burden.

execution_run.what happened: Moved planning-module tiny report text from generated facade-local helper output into the codegen-owned output.emit primitive, regenerated Memory/Planning/Workspace runtime facades to call that policy, and added static accepted runtime/output-emission boundary metrics to the Python completion blocker report.
execution_run.scope touched: #1080 output policy migration; command-generation primitive executor and generator; generated runtime facades; runtime projection inventory; checker metrics; primitive conformance test.
execution_run.validations run: generate --check; primitive conformance; generated package static proof; Python conformance; Docker conformance; completion blocker metrics; contract tooling; structured inventory; targeted ruff; tests/test_workspace_cli.py; make lint-workspace; make test-workspace

finished_run_review.scope respected: Scope stayed inside the #1080 output-policy slice. #1079 remains open for later child slices; this slice satisfied the first output policy migration by moving one reusable text family into codegen-owned output policy and proving generated parity.
